mnemonics.title: CAP Theorem Remember
mnemonics.content: CAP: Choose Availability during Partition (or Consistency) - remember 'P for Partition forces C or A choice'

mnemonics.title: Uptime Quick Calc
mnemonics.content: Nines: 1=3.65 days, 2=8.76 hours, 3=52.6 minutes, 4=5.26 minutes downtime/year

acronyms.usage: Contract defining uptime and performance guarantees
Maximum acceptable time to restore service after outage
Maximum acceptable data loss measured in time
Provides automatic router failover for high availability

visualTips.visualization: Think of a highway: latency is travel time from point A to B, throughput is number of cars passing a point per minute. A short highway with one lane has low latency but low throughput.
Vertical: building a taller building on same land. Horizontal: building more buildings on more land. Horizontal provides better fault tolerance.
Triangle with C, A, P at corners. During partition (network issue), you can only reach two corners - choose between Consistent or Available.
Availability = MTBF / (MTBF + MTTR). Picture a seesaw: higher MTBF (reliability) and lower MTTR (repair time) raises the availability percentage.

analogies.analogy: Latency is like the delay in hearing an echo - the time between shouting and hearing your voice return, regardless of how loud (bandwidth) your voice is.
A load balancer is like a skilled traffic police officer at an intersection, directing cars to different routes to prevent any single road from becoming congested.
Failover is like an automatic backup generator in a hospital - when main power fails, backup instantly takes over without any interruption to life-support systems.
Redundancy is like having spare tires in a car. If one goes flat, you have another ready to use immediately, ensuring you reach your destination.
Edge computing is like having a local clinic in your neighborhood instead of traveling to a distant hospital for every minor health issue - faster response, less travel.
SLA is like a warranty on a product - it guarantees certain performance standards and specifies what happens if those standards aren't met.
